title: [Bug]: JSON label array items can contain the semicolon storage separator
summary: Configured labels supplied as JSON arrays can contain semicolons, which makes scalar storage ambiguous because labels are persisted as semicolon-separated strings in relational columns.
observed: A configured label list such as ['alpha;beta', 'gamma'] normalizes successfully and later renders indistinguishably from three separate labels in scalar storage.
expected: Configured label values should reject the semicolon separator when labels are supplied as individual values.
reproduction: Construct MessageMetadataLabelsConfig(header='Labels', default=['alpha;beta']) and observe validation succeeds.
failing_test: Add a unit test that expects a configured label array item containing ';' to fail validation.
impact: Ambiguous label storage can confuse filtering, audit review, and downstream reconciliation.
delivery_semantics: No ACK behavior changes. The fix only rejects ambiguous configuration.
security: Rejecting ambiguous metadata values supports auditability and avoids downstream parser confusion.
acceptance: Configured label list items reject the semicolon separator.
Semicolon-separated string input remains supported and is split into individual labels.
The regression test proves ambiguous JSON array label items fail closed.
Documentation or release notes mention the stricter label configuration rule.
tests: Run focused metadata boundary tests and full validation.
closeout: Mark completed after merge with sanitized evidence.
